_notes: Hand-verified 2026-04-24. INFS3822 Assessment Guide T1 2026. Course code/name/term explicit. No teachers or textbook in extracted front-matter.

assessmentItems.name: Assessment 1: Individual Assignment 1
assessmentItems.format: Hands-on AI analysis using Python or R

assessmentItems.name: Assessment 2: Individual Assignment 2
assessmentItems.format: Designing an AI solution for Gumtree Australia

assessmentItems.name: Assessment 3: Team Project
assessmentItems.dueDate: Team Agreement: Friday, 20 March, 2026, 5:00pm (Week 5); Team report & pitch deck: Monday, 20 April, 2026, 5:00pm (Week 10); Individual reflection: Monday, 27 April, 2026, 5:00pm (Week 11)
assessmentItems.format: Implementing and evaluating an AI proof-of-concept for Gumtree Australia

learningObjectives: Identify and apply AI-driven business analytics technologies, their underlying concepts and business applications.
Consolidate analytical and critical thinking skills to solve real-world business problems using AI-driven solutions.
Explain the rationale behind the application of supervised and unsupervised predictive models to analyse structured and unstructured data, to solve real-world organisational problems.
Communicate effectively the business problem at hand, explain the outcomes of the predictive models generated by machine learning algorithms, and unravel actionable insights.

Discuss ethical implications aligned with SDGs (Sustainable Development Goals) principles/objectives when implementing AI-driven analytics and articulate their impacts on individuals, organisations and society at large.
Develop collaborative teamwork skills for solving AI-driven business problems.
